criteria.label: State which curriculum topic owns one-hot, ordinal, and target encoding definitions versus pipeline workflow.
criteria.example: Write two sentences: Encoding Categoricals (preprocessing) owns definitions and pitfalls; feature-engineering Encoding owns ColumnTransformer workflow and cardinality decisions.

criteria.label: Sketch a ColumnTransformer with one nominal and one numeric branch.
criteria.example: Pseudocode: ColumnTransformer([('cat', OneHotEncoder(), ['plan']), ('num', StandardScaler(), ['age'])]) fit on X_train, transform X_val.

criteria.label: Recommend encoding strategies for columns with different cardinality on the same dataset.
criteria.example: Given `region` (8 levels) and `sku` (30k levels), propose one-hot for region and top-500 bucketing plus frequency encoding for sku with one-line justification each.

criteria.label: Explain why the full Pipeline must be serialized for production.
criteria.example: Describe a failure mode where serving only LogisticRegression without OneHotEncoder causes column count mismatch on new `plan` values.

criteria.label: Design a rare-level bucketing rule before target encoding a high-cardinality column.
criteria.example: For `merchant_id`, keep top 500 by training count, map others to `__other__`, then note that CV target encoding applies to the bucketed column—not raw 40k ids.
